So the code would look something like:

public long minimumCost(String s) {
    int n = s.length();
    long total = 0;
    for (int i = 0; i < n - 1; i++) {
        if (s.charAt(i) != s.charAt(i+1)) {
            int cost1 = i + 1;
            int cost2 = n - (i + 1);
            total += Math.min(cost1, cost2);
        }
    }
    return total;
}

class Solution {
    public long minimumCost(String s) {
        int n = s.length();
        long total = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < n - 1; i++) {
            if (s.charAt(i) != s.charAt(i + 1)) {
                int cost1 = i + 1;
                int cost2 = n - (i + 1);
                total += Math.min(cost1, cost2);
            }
        }
        return total;
    }
}

solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public long minimumCost(String s) {
        int n = s.length();
        long total = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < n - 1; i++) {
            if (s.charAt(i) != s.charAt(i + 1)) {
                int cost1 = i + 1;
                int cost2 = n - (i + 1);
                total += Math.min(cost1, cost2);
            }
        }
        return total;
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution identifies transitions between adjacent characters in the binary string. For each transition, it calculates the minimum cost between flipping the prefix up to that point or the suffix starting just after. Summing these minima ensures optimal cost. The approach is efficient with linear time and constant space.
